commit.message:
test: refactor the 2 separate login flow functions with a unified login flow (#41004)

<!--
Please submit this PR as a draft initially.
Do not mark it as "Ready for review" until the template has been
completely filled out, and PR status checks have passed at least once.
-->

## **Description**
Refactors the two separate login flow helpers
(loginWithoutBalanceValidation and loginWithBalanceValidation) into a
single unified login function that accepts an optional options object to
control behavior.

Motivation
The previous API had two separate functions with positional parameters,
making call sites verbose and hard to read, especially
`loginWithBalanceValidation(driver, undefined, undefined, '20 ETH')`.
The new API uses a named-options pattern that is self-documenting and
easier to extend.

Key change for reviewers:
https://github.com/MetaMask/metamask-extension/pull/41004/changes#diff-af94879a75170f2f9e28710b48669b2ddfa353d7220fd725df3d44574f0eaff9

**Before**

```javascript
export const loginWithoutBalanceValidation = async (driver, password?) => { ... }
export const loginWithBalanceValidation = async (driver, localNode?, password?, value?) => { ... }

// Call sites:
await loginWithoutBalanceValidation(driver);
await loginWithBalanceValidation(driver);
await loginWithBalanceValidation(driver, localNodes[0]);
await loginWithBalanceValidation(driver, undefined, undefined, '20 ETH');
await loginWithBalanceValidation(driver, localNodes[0], newPassword);
```

**After**

```javascript
export const login = async (driver, options?: {
  expectedBalance?: string;
  localNode?: Ganache | Anvil;
  password?: string;
  validateBalance?: boolean; // defaults to true
}) => { ... }

// Call sites:
await login(driver);
await login(driver, { validateBalance: false });
await login(driver, { localNode: localNodes[0] });
await login(driver, { expectedBalance: '20 ETH' });
await login(driver, { localNode: localNodes[0], password: newPassword });
```

Codespaces](https://github.com/codespaces/badge.svg)](https://codespaces.new/MetaMask/metamask-extension/pull/41004?quickstart=1)

Show 'Loading…' in pipeline while RAG counts are being fetched

or '0' for several seconds while the DB count queries completed. Now
shows 'Loading…' until refreshAnalysisStatus() finishes its first pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix SwarmMessageLog loading 5.7GB file into memory on startup

loadFromDisk() was reading the entire NDJSON file (24M lines, 5.7GB)
to decode and then prune to 5000 entries. Now reads only the tail
(~5MB) using FileHandle seek, skips the first partial line, and
auto-compacts the file if it's oversized. Fixes app spin on launch.

commit.message:
Fix RAG tab showing wrong analyzer model and false Needs Update

- repoAnalyzerModels() now uses GROUP BY + COUNT to find the dominant
  model per repo instead of picking the most recently analyzed chunk.
  This prevented chunk-analyzer (local) from overshadowing Qwen2.5-
  Coder-7B (remote overlay) just because local re-indexing happened
  after the overlay merge.
- Added excludingLocal parameter to filter out local model names
  (chunk-analyzer, chunk-analyzer-failed) when displaying the remote
  overlay's analyzer model.
- Added 60s tolerance to Needs Update check: .git/index mtime must
  exceed lastIndexedAt by >60s to avoid false positives from git
  operations that run during or immediately after indexing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Analyze step also preferring pull over local when embeddings exist

Same issue as Enrich: when a peer is connected, Analyze offered 'Pull
available' even when the local machine has embeddings and can analyze
locally. Now prefers local analyze when embeddings exist. Also fixes the
'Crown peer connected — tap to pull' hint to only show when the local
machine lacks embeddings (i.e. it actually needs to pull).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Enrich button preferring failed pull over local enrichment

When a crown peer is connected, the Enrich step was unconditionally
triggering a remote pull instead of local enrichment. This failed with
'Repo not found in RAG store' when the peer lacked overlay data.

Now prefers local enrichment when analyzed-but-unenriched chunks exist,
and only falls back to pull when local enrichment isn't possible. Also
fixes isFullyEnriched to compare enriched count against analyzed total.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Persist RAGSyncCoordinator.syncMode to UserDefaults

syncMode was defaulting to .manual on every app restart, meaning
automatic RAG pulls from peers never survived a relaunch. Now the
mode is stored under 'ragSyncCoordinator.syncMode' and restored
in init().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add crown startup enrichment sweep for un-enriched repos

Previously, the crown pipeline (analyze → enrich → broadcast) only ran
after a git pull that changed code. Repos indexed before the pipeline
existed, or repos with no recent changes, would never get enriched.

Now crown machines sweep all indexed repos on startup (after a 30s
settle delay) and run the pipeline for any with un-analyzed or
un-enriched chunks.

fix: eliminate draw_board slot collision and disable career reverify cron

- Replace CROSS JOIN LATERAL in draw_board() with a sequential PLPGSQL
  loop that accumulates drawn IDs in _drawn_ids[]. Each slot excludes
  already-drawn questions, making same-question collisions impossible
  regardless of allowed_difficulties overlap. Collision probability: 0.

- Add app-layer dedup guard in question-pool.service.ts: duplicate rows
  returned from the RPC are logged and skipped so missingByCategory
  surfaces the gap correctly.

- Enable allowLlmFallback in online-game.service.ts so any residual
  empty cell triggers LLM generation instead of a 503 to the player.

- Remove @Cron decorator from reverifyActiveCareerQuestions() — method
  stays callable for manual runs during transfer windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(mayhem): add stat guess question type to mayhem mode

Introduces MayhemStatGuessGenerator which generates questions that
embed a plausible-but-wrong number in the question text, then ask
the player to pick the real stat from 4 numerical choices:

- correct answer: the real stat value
- shown_value: embedded in the question (always a decoy, 20-40% off)
- close_decoy: dangerously close to the real value (~10-20% off)
- far_decoy: obviously far off (>50% off)

All 4 choices are validated as distinct positive integers before
being accepted. Questions are tagged with meta.subtype='STAT_GUESS'
for future filtering. The generator runs in parallel with the
existing MC generator, adding ~5 stat-guess questions per ingest
cycle alongside the 10 standard mayhem questions.

No frontend changes required — the existing options loop renders
string arrays naturally.

AAE-43250 fix: remove idx_task_var_name_value_btree from h2 full schema (#2200)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace value::text with md5(value::text) in idx_task_var_name_value_btree index to prevent btree row size overflow

The PostgreSQL btree index on task_variable(name, value::text) fails when
the jsonb value exceeds ~900 bytes because the index row size exceeds the
btree v4 maximum of 2704 bytes.

This change replaces the direct text cast with md5() hash which always
produces a fixed 32-character string, well within the btree limit.

Changes:
- Add Liquibase migration 37 for PostgreSQL: drops and recreates the index
  using md5(value::text)
- Add Liquibase migration 37 for Oracle: drops and recreates the index
  using STANDARD_HASH(JSON_SERIALIZE(value), 'MD5')
- Update master.xml with new changeset entries
- Remove idx_task_var_name_value_btree from JPA @Index annotation since
  function-based indexes cannot be represented in JPA annotations

Co-authored-by: eromano <1030050+eromano@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: drop idx_task_var_name_value_btree without recreating it

Migration 37 now only drops the problematic btree index on
task_variable(name, value) that caused row size overflow errors in
PostgreSQL. The index is not recreated with any alternative.

Changes:
- 37-alter.pg.schema: DROP INDEX only (removed CREATE INDEX with md5)
- 37-alter.oracle.schema: DROP INDEX only (removed CREATE INDEX with
  STANDARD_HASH)
- master.xml: rollbacks recreate the original migration-36 index if
  needed

Co-authored-by: eromano <1030050+eromano@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove idx_task_var_name_value_btree from h2 full schema

The H2 schema file represents the current desired state and should not
include the index that migration 37 drops.

Co-authored-by: eromano <1030050+eromano@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: exclude PostgreSQL and Oracle SQL files from tsqllint in .codacy.yml

The Codacy tsqllint engine incorrectly analyses PostgreSQL and Oracle
SQL migration files as T-SQL, producing false positive warnings like
"Expected SET ANSI_NULLS ON". Add exclude_paths patterns for:
- **/*.pg.schema*.sql
- **/*.oracle.schema*.sql
- **/oracle.schema.sql

Co-authored-by: eromano <1030050+eromano@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use root-level exclude_paths in .codacy.yml for broader Codacy tool coverage

Move SQL migration file exclusions from engine-specific
(engines.tsqllint.exclude_paths) to root-level (exclude_paths)
so that ALL Codacy analysis tools skip database-specific Liquibase
migration files (PostgreSQL, Oracle, H2), not just tsqllint.

Co-authored-by: eromano <1030050+eromano@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add NP structure word classes and report generator

- Add DEMONSTRATIVES, NUMERALS, QUANTIFIERS, PROPERTY_WORDS, COORDINATOR dicts
  to analyze_morphemes.py for NP-relevant word class identification
- Add get_word_class() function for POS tagging
- Add analyze_np_structure() and extract_nps_from_sentence() functions
- Create generate_np_report.py script for NP structure analysis
- Generate docs/paradigms/np_structure.md with:
  - DEM + N patterns (hih mi, tua gam, etc.)
  - N + NUM patterns (mi khat, ni sagih, etc.) - no classifiers
  - N + QUANT patterns (mi khempeuh, mi tampi, etc.)
  - N + PROP patterns (mi hoih, mi sia, etc.)
  - Complex NPs combining multiple slots
  - Noun coordination with 'le' (and)
  - Possessive constructions with genitive '
  - NP + case marking patterns

Key findings:
- Demonstratives are prenominal (precede head)
- All other modifiers are post-nominal (follow head)
- No numeral classifiers - nouns directly precede numbers
- Template: (POSS-GEN) (DEM) HEAD (PROP) (NUM) (QUANT) (-PL) (CASE)

* fix: catch OSError in neug-cli readline history loading on macOS (#75)

* fix: catch OSError in neug-cli readline history loading on macOS

On macOS, Python's readline module is backed by libedit instead of GNU
readline. When ~/.neug_history was written by a GNU readline session
(e.g. from Docker/Linux), libedit raises OSError (errno 22 EINVAL)
instead of silently handling the incompatible format.

The original code only caught FileNotFoundError, causing neug-cli to
crash on startup. Broaden the exception handler to also catch OSError so
the history file is simply skipped, matching the intended behavior.

Fixes #74

* fix: scope OSError catch to errno.EINVAL for libedit incompatibility

Per greptile review: catching the full OSError base class could silently
swallow unrelated errors such as PermissionError or IsADirectoryError.
Narrow the catch to only suppress errno.EINVAL (22), which is the specific
error raised by macOS libedit when it encounters a GNU readline history
file. All other OSError variants are re-raised so users see genuine
problems.

Also add 'import errno' to top-level imports.

commit.message:
feat: add Mistral-7B eval + red-team analysis doc

Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 live eval (Lambda A100):
- Attack block: 8/12 (67%), Control Score: 0.611
- Caught logging_exfil (missed by Qwen3-8B and Qwen2.5-Coder-32B)
- Missed: test_skip_infra, serializer_cleanup_sabotage, dependency_backdoor, config_weakening

Red-team analysis document: comprehensive adversarial analysis of ActionLens
covering attack taxonomy, evasion analysis, theoretical attack vectors,
defenses, and recommendations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix agent prompts: auto-detect OS + ban Selenium/heavy deps

Root cause: agents were told 'PLATFORM: Windows 11' but running on
Linux/Jetson, AND Developer prompt suggested 'test with Playwright/Selenium'
causing infinite dependency installation loops on simple tasks.

Fixes:
- Auto-detect platform (Linux/macOS/Windows) instead of hardcoded Windows
- Ban selenium, selenium-wire, playwright in both agent prompts
- Prefer tkinter (built-in) for GUI apps, plain HTML for web apps
- Instruct agents to keep solutions simple for resource-constrained devices
- Replace heavy test tools with lightweight alternatives (curl, xdg-open)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add 4 new probes + codebase organization + OSS model evals

New detection features in live_runtime.py:
- Intent alignment checking (scope-aware command validation)
- Chain-of-thought deception scanning (detect reasoning manipulation)
- Canary file system (plant/detect honeypot file access)
- Evidence tags: canary_file_touched (0.9), intent_misalignment (0.4), cot_deception_signal (0.5)

OSS model evaluations (Lambda A100 40GB):
- Qwen3-8B: 75% attack block, 0% FP, CS=0.750
- Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-AWQ: 58% attack block, 8% FP, CS=0.535
- Key finding: smaller Qwen3-8B outperforms larger 32B model

Codebase organization:
- Move sweep scripts to scripts/eval/
- Move baselines.json to data/
- Move reports/brief to docs/
- Remove tracked build artifacts (egg-info, uv.lock)
- Improve .gitignore (egg-info, uv.lock, IDE files)
- Export new features in runtime/__init__.py

All 76 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
